rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITIONER RAMESES JOHNSON'S AMENDED NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR RELEASE OF PROPERTY FROM LIEN
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 5, 2022 line 1. PETITIONER RAMESES JOHNSON'S AMENDED NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR RELEASE OF PROPERTY FROM LIEN is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Moving party to prepare order. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARY RAGO VS. WOOD STREET PLAZA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement And To Modify Judgment Entered Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 5, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT SAMSON LAI, SUSAN LAI, YOSEMITE CAPITAL, LLC, ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement And To Modify Judgment Entered Pursuant To Settlement Agreement is GRANTED. Defendants to prepare an amended judgment reflecting that title reverts to the original owner subject to the Yosemite Parties' 2017 Deed of Trust. (Judgment, paragraph 2). Request for sanctions per the terms of the settlement agreement is denied. This motion could have been avoided by a simple review of the proposed judgment. Request for sanctions under CCP 128.5 is not considered on this motion and must be properly made in accordance with CCP 128.5 procedures. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: XIAN YU ZHAO VS. XIANG FEI BAI, ALL OTHERS PERSONS CLAIMING A RIGHT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moton And Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Responses, Without Objections, To Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, Request For Admission; And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Xiang Fei Bai
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 5, 2022 line 4. PLAINTIFF XIAN ZHAO Notice Of Moton And Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Responses, Without Objections, To Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, Request For Admission; And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Xiang Fei Bai is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Trial is continued to December 19, 2022 for Plaintiff to receive the responses and prepare for trial. Sanctions granted in the amount of $850. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
